name: Heian Dai Shogi
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Heian Dai Shogi (平安大将棋): the earliest known large Shogi variant, described in the 12th-century Nichureki. 13x13 board, 34 pieces a side in 13 types -- all simple movers (the Flying Dragon is a bishop, the Free Chariot a vertical rook). No drops; almost everything promotes to a Gold General in the far three ranks. Win by checkmate or by reducing the opponent to a bare King.
